context.docs: CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (automation workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline)
env: Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo + Vitest + strict TS. Run from repo root. Node 20+.

Real Postgres (dev): DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). Single test file: pnpm --filter <pkg> exec vitest run path. Whole suite: DATABASE_URL=… pnpm test. Typecheck: pnpm typecheck. Lint: pnpm lint.

Browser e2e: pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e — its OWN DB cdp_e2e on its OWN ports (local-api :8788, web :5174), runs with LOCAL_SES_FORCE_MOCK=1 LOCAL_SES_DKIM_STATUS=SUCCESS; re-seeds web/e2e/seed.ts each run. The dev stack (cdp, :8787/:5173) coexists. Playwright config has retries:1.
Dev API for manual checks: pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api (background, restart after backend edits). Health: GET http://localhost:8787/health.

Key reuse points: services/automation-runner (dsl.ts, core.ts, run.ts, enroll.ts), services/local-api (handlers.ts, routes.ts, app.ts, server.ts), services/dispatcher (core.ts, dispatch.ts), packages/segments (AST + compiler + rule builder), packages/email, web/src/screens (BroadcastComposer email-instance flow, TemplateEditor, SegmentBuilder), web/src/ui/kit.tsx (ActionMenu, Drawer, Button, Field, Select), web/e2e/seed.ts.
invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL and every query filters by workspace_id. The automation-runner + enrollment + handlers connect with the Supabase SERVICE ROLE which BYPASSES RLS — they MUST scope by workspace_id IN CODE (RLS is not their safety net). workspace_id is loaded FROM the enrollment/automation row, never assumed.

workspace_id is NEVER client-supplied — it comes from the authorizer-injected claim (admin API) or a trusted server-set field (the enrollment row). Never trust a workspace_id in a request body. Reject a cross-workspace template_id / segment_id / sender_id (inv.2) — like validateSenderId does today.

All automation action SENDS go through the SAME outbox → Dispatcher pipeline as broadcasts (§9): suppression → frequency-cap → quiet-hours, gated on a VERIFIED sending domain. A send node carries an email INSTANCE whose envelope (real named From sender + To + Subject) is ALL mandatory — NO no-reply fallback (mirror sendBroadcast gating). The Dispatcher renders merge tags in the SUBJECT, To AND body.

Runner idempotency + concurrency (unchanged guarantee): one tick advances a due enrollment exactly once (SELECT … FOR UPDATE row lock in one tx, re-check active inside the lock; legacy CAS path for unit tests). Outbox dedupe_key per (automation_id, profile_id, node_id) prevents double-sends on retry. MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK guards pathological graphs. New node execution must preserve all of this.

Webhook action safety: outbound HTTP ONLY to an explicit per-workspace domain ALLOWLIST; enforce a timeout + BOUNDED retries; block SSRF (no internal/link-local/loopback/169.254 metadata targets); secrets (e.g. auth headers) use the @cdp/db secret-crypto envelope pattern, never returned in plaintext over the API; a webhook failure is ISOLATED (recorded; never crashes the tick or double-advances). LOCAL: the HTTP call is mocked/injected — never hit a real external host in tests.

Workspace timezone governs ALL automation time math (waits, wait-until, hour-of-day window) — DST-correct (reuse the zoned↔UTC approach from the broadcast scheduler: zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs). One configurable timezone per workspace; never per-broadcast guesswork.

The DSL ({startNode, nodes}) is the SINGLE graph model; validateAutomationDefinition is the structural gate (exactly ONE trigger, every edge resolves, a reachable exit, and — extended — NO cycles / NO orphan nodes / down-only). The builder is an AUTO-LAYOUT RENDER of that graph: compute (x,y) from the tree, draw rounded orthogonal connectors, branches fan sideways. NO separate graph model, NO stored coordinates, NO loops/back-edges.

REUSE, do not reinvent (code-quality bar): the broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone flow (kind=copy, source_template_id, cloneTemplate, the From/To/Subject envelope + editor return flow) for send nodes; the segment rule builder + AstNode for the if/condition node; the kit ActionMenu / Drawer / Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) / Field / Select; the Dispatcher core; scopedQuery; runInWorkspaceTx; evaluateRealtimeSegmentsForProfile. Match the surrounding code style, comment density and naming.

UI standing rules: NEVER use native confirm/alert/prompt — use ui/dialog.tsx (askConfirm) or purpose-built modals. EVERY server-calling button shows a spinner + locks until the response (kit Button auto-locks when onClick RETURNS the promise — wire it that way). The email editor autosaves (no manual save). PRESERVE every existing data-testid; add stable testids for new UI. Floating toasts (ui/toast.tsx showToast) for transient feedback.

Test-first (§16A/§18): write the failing acceptance tests FIRST, then implement to green. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the REAL local DB. Mock SES/AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack); the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. Playwright (real browser) for UI phases with seeded data + stable selectors. turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys).

Process hygiene: BUMP the root package.json version per change (patch=tiny, minor=feature, major only when told). UPDATE CLAUDE.md when behavior/architecture changes. Apply new SQL migrations to BOTH the dev DB (cdp) AND the e2e DB (cdp_e2e) via a node pg script (psql is unavailable). RESTART the dev API (pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api — a long-lived tsx process, NOT watch) after backend edits, and REBUILD a changed package (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist. Scope discipline: build EXACTLY the agreed design — no extra features.
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp). Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack; the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. No frontend.

criteria: Workspace timezone is persisted + read back via the settings API (workspace-scoped, owner-gated like other settings); an invalid IANA zone is rejected.

validateAutomationDefinition accepts a well-formed graph using the new hour_of_day_window + webhook nodes, and REJECTS: a missing/invalid window range, a webhook missing url/method, a cycle/back-edge, an orphan node, more than one trigger, an unreachable exit. All covered by passing unit tests.
The DSL types are exported + documented; no runner/execution change yet (that is phase 2). Typecheck + lint clean; no regression in existing automation-runner unit tests.
phase.refs: §9B + CLAUDE.md (automations DSL, workspace settings, broadcast scheduler tz math)
scope: Add a WORKSPACE TIMEZONE setting: persist on workspaces.settings.timezone (or a column) with a sane default (UTC); read/write via the existing workspace settings API (PUT /workspace/settings) + a small picker in WorkspaceSettings.tsx (full IANA list via Intl.supportedValuesOf, like the broadcast schedule tz). This is the clock for all automation time math.

Extend the automation DSL (services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts) with TWO new node kinds, PURE + structurally validated: (a) an hour_of_day_window node — config: allowed hour range (startHour..endHour, 0–23) + optional allowed days-of-week; advances when already inside the window, else parks until the next window opening (computed later in the runner). (b) a webhook ACTION kind on the action node — config: url, method, headers (incl. an optional secret-backed auth header), bodyTemplate (merge-aware), timeoutMs, maxRetries; next edge after the call.

Extend validateAutomationDefinition for the new nodes (required fields + edges) AND tighten it to the builder invariants: reject CYCLES (back-edges) and ORPHAN nodes (every node reachable from startNode), in addition to the existing one-trigger / reachable-exit checks. Keep it pure + exhaustively unit-tested.
phase.title: Workspace timezone + DSL extensions (hour-window, webhook)

e2eFlows: NONE this phase (phase.frontend=false; the WorkspaceSettings picker is covered by a component unit test, not Playwright). A browser e2e for the timezone picker + automation builder is deferred to a later UI phase. If a smoke e2e is desired, it would be: sign in as owner (seeded Acme) -> Workspace settings -> select a timezone -> reload -> assert it persisted; explicitly out of scope now.
implUnits: 1. (test-first) Write/extend services/automation-runner/test/node-dsl-and-validation.test.ts with the new accept/reject cases AND fix the now-cyclic 'reachable exit' fixture; add dsl-types-exports.test.ts. Run -> RED.

2. Extend services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts: add HourOfDayWindowNode interface (type:'hour_of_day_window', startHour, endHour, daysOfWeek?, next), add 'webhook' to ActionNode.kind with url/method/headers/bodyTemplate/timeoutMs/maxRetries fields (document overnight wrap-around + that secret auth headers will be envelope-encrypted at rest in phase 2), add both to the Node union and NODE_TYPES set, add validateNodeFields arms, add edgesOf arm.

3. Add detectCycle + findOrphans pure helpers and call them inside validateAutomationDefinition (after one-trigger, per-node, startNode-resolves, reachable-exit). Update the function's doc comment to list the new checks. Re-export new types via services/automation-runner/src/index.ts. Run unit tests -> GREEN.

4. Create packages/shared/src/timezone.ts by EXTRACTING tzOffsetMs/zonedInputToUtcIso/utcIsoToZonedInput/timeZoneList from BroadcastComposer + add isValidTimeZone; export from packages/shared/src/index.ts. Add packages/shared/test/timezone.test.ts. Re-import the helpers in web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx (delete the local copies) — assert no behavior change. Run -> GREEN. (Rebuild @cdp/shared via tsc -b since local-api imports from dist.)

6. Extend services/local-api/src/handlers.ts: getWorkspaceSettings adds timezone default 'UTC'; updateWorkspaceSettings adds a timezone branch that validates via isValidTimeZone (from @cdp/shared) and 400s on invalid, else merges into the settings jsonb (reuse the existing settings || $2::jsonb pattern, workspace_id from ctx only). Route stays 'manage_workspace_users'. Run integration -> GREEN. RESTART dev API after the edit.

7. Write web/test/workspace-timezone-picker.test.ts -> RED; add the picker to WorkspaceSettings.tsx (a kit Select fed by timeZoneList(), initialized from GET, saved via PUT with optimistic rollback + showToast, promise-returning save so the kit Button auto-locks; new stable testids). Run -> GREEN.

8. pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint clean; run the FULL automation-runner unit suite + local-api + web unit suites to confirm no regression. Bump root package.json (minor — new feature). Update CLAUDE.md (automations DSL section: two new node kinds + tightened validator; workspace settings: timezone setting; note tz helpers now live in @cdp/shared).
mocks: No SES/AWS in this phase (no sends executed yet) — none needed for the DSL/settings tests.
No webhook HTTP client invoked in this phase (types + structural validation only); the injected/mocked HTTP client is a PHASE-2 concern — explicitly NOT mocked here.
web picker test: mock the api client (api.get/api.put) — no real network.
@cdp/shared/timezone fallback test: stub Intl.supportedValuesOf to undefined to exercise the curated fallback.
plan.notes: PLANNING ONLY — no code/tests written here. Two gotchas the implementer must handle: (1) the EXISTING 'requires a reachable exit' unit test uses a wait node that loops back to the trigger (w.next='t'); once cycle-rejection lands, that graph throws /cycle/ before /no exit/, so the test MUST be rewritten to an acyclic-but-exitless graph — otherwise it's a false regression. (2) Cycle detection must NOT flag a legitimate diamond / re-convergence (two paths into the same downstream node) — use a DFS grey-stack (back-edge to a node on the current recursion stack), not a global 'seen' set; add an explicit diamond-accept test. Scope discipline: hour_of_day_window + webhook are TYPES + STRUCTURAL validation ONLY — NO runner execution, NO real HTTP, NO SSRF IP/host allowlist enforcement, NO secret encryption wiring (all phase 2); the only SSRF-adjacent guard here is the http(s)-scheme check in the validator. Overnight windows (startHour>endHour) are accepted as valid wrap-around (document semantics; runner resolves later). The tz extraction must be behavior-preserving (lock exact ISO outputs in the shared tz test). Apply no new SQL migration — timezone lives in the existing workspaces.settings jsonb. Bump root version (minor) and update CLAUDE.md per process hygiene. Tier mix: pure unit (DSL + tz helpers + picker component), REAL-Postgres integration (settings API), no e2e this phase.
pureFunctions: validateAutomationDefinition (EXTEND): add validation arms for hour_of_day_window (startHour/endHour integer 0-23, optional daysOfWeek subset of 0-6 unique non-empty, next edge) and action kind 'webhook' (url http(s) only, method in {GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE}, headers object, bodyTemplate string, timeoutMs>0, maxRetries>=0, next edge).
validateNodeFields (EXTEND): new switch cases for the two node shapes; webhook url scheme guard (reject non-http(s)) as a model-layer SSRF pre-check.
edgesOf (EXTEND): return [node.next] for hour_of_day_window; webhook action already covered by the existing 'action' arm returning [node.next].
hasReachableExit (KEEP): unchanged BFS — already traverses via edgesOf.

detectCycle (NEW, pure): DFS with a recursion/grey-stack over the node graph from startNode; throws on a back-edge to a node currently on the DFS stack (distinguish from a diamond re-convergence which is allowed).

findOrphans (NEW, pure): BFS/DFS reachable-set from startNode; any node id in nodes not in the reachable set is an orphan -> throw. Compose both into validateAutomationDefinition after the per-node + reachable-exit checks.
isValidTimeZone (NEW, @cdp/shared): true iff new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-US',{timeZone}) does not throw; used by the settings API and the picker.

tzOffsetMs / zonedInputToUtcIso / utcIsoToZonedInput / timeZoneList (EXTRACT from web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx into @cdp/shared/timezone.ts): move verbatim, re-import in BroadcastComposer so automation time math reuses the SAME DST-correct implementation. Behavior-preserving extraction.
reuse: services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts — extend in place (Node union, NODE_TYPES, validateNodeFields, edgesOf, validateAutomationDefinition, hasReachableExit). Do NOT introduce a second graph model (invariant: DSL is the single graph model).
services/automation-runner/test/node-dsl-and-validation.test.ts validDef() helper + existing assertion style — extend it; reuse @cdp/segments AstNode in the condition node (already imported).

web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx tz helpers (tzOffsetMs/zonedInputToUtcIso/utcIsoToZonedInput/timeZoneList) — extract to @cdp/shared/timezone.ts and reuse on BOTH sides (broadcast scheduler + automation time math) so there is ONE DST-correct implementation.

services/local-api/src/handlers.ts get/updateWorkspaceSettings — extend the existing settings jsonb merge (lowercase_emails/link_tracking pattern); routes.ts capability mapping ('manage_workspace_users') unchanged.

web/src/screens/WorkspaceSettings.tsx existing toggle pattern (optimistic state + savingSettings lock + rollback on catch) for the timezone picker; web/src/ui/kit.tsx Select + Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) + Field; web/src/ui/toast.tsx showToast for feedback (never native alert/confirm).

packages/db/src/secret-crypto.ts (encryptSecret/decryptSecret/isEncryptedSecret) — referenced in the webhook field DOC/comment as the phase-2 mechanism for the optional auth header secret; NOT wired this phase (types only).
packages/shared/src/index.ts barrel + project-references (tsc -b) so the new timezone module is importable by web AND local-api.

assertions: EXTEND existing file (do not replace) — keep validDef() + every current passing assertion green (one-trigger, unresolvable edge, missing startNode, per-type required fields, unknown type, resolveStartNode/findNode).

FIX the current 'requires a reachable exit' test: its `w: wait -> t` is now a CYCLE, so it would throw /cycle/ not /no exit/. Replace with a genuinely no-exit-but-acyclic graph (e.g. t->a(set_attribute)->a2(set_attribute)->... all chain to nodes that never reach an exit, or a chain ending at a node whose next is itself-forbidden) OR a chain t->w(until)->a where a.next points only back UP is removed; use a finite acyclic chain that simply contains no exit node at all.
ACCEPTS a well-formed graph containing an hour_of_day_window node: { type:'hour_of_day_window', startHour:9, endHour:17, next:'a' } wired trigger->window->send->exit, not toThrow.
ACCEPTS hour_of_day_window with optional daysOfWeek: [1,2,3,4,5] (Mon-Fri); not toThrow.
REJECTS hour_of_day_window missing startHour or endHour -> toThrow(/startHour|endHour|hour window/).
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with out-of-range hours (startHour:-1, endHour:24, or non-integer 9.5) -> toThrow(/hour/).
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with an invalid daysOfWeek (value 7, duplicate, non-array, empty array) -> toThrow(/day/).

ACCEPTS a window where startHour > endHour (overnight window, e.g. 22..6) as a VALID wrap-around range (document the semantics; runner interprets later) — assert not toThrow, so the structural gate does not forbid it.
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with a missing/unresolvable next edge -> toThrow(/next|unresolvable/).

ACCEPTS an action node with kind:'webhook' fully configured { kind:'webhook', url:'https://hooks.example.com/x', method:'POST', headers:{}, bodyTemplate:'{}', timeoutMs:5000, maxRetries:2, next:'x' } -> not toThrow.
REJECTS webhook action missing url -> toThrow(/url/).
REJECTS webhook action missing/invalid method (not one of GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) -> toThrow(/method/).

REJECTS webhook action with a non-http(s) url scheme (e.g. file:, ftp:, javascript:) -> toThrow(/url|scheme|https?/) — structural SSRF pre-check at the model layer (full IP/host allowlist SSRF enforcement is phase 2 runtime, but the scheme guard belongs here).
REJECTS webhook action with a non-object headers, negative/zero timeoutMs, or negative maxRetries -> toThrow.
REJECTS a CYCLE / back-edge: trigger->w(wait)->c(condition onTrue:'w' onFalse:'x') where an edge points back to an ancestor -> toThrow(/cycle|back-edge/).
REJECTS a self-loop: a node whose own next targets itself -> toThrow(/cycle/).
REJECTS an ORPHAN node: a graph with a valid reachable path to an exit PLUS an extra node not reachable from startNode -> toThrow(/orphan|unreachable|not reachable/).

Confirms a diamond (condition onTrue->m, onFalse->n, both m,n -> same exit) is NOT a cycle and is ACCEPTED (a node reached via two paths is fine; only a back-edge to an ancestor is a cycle) — guards against a naive 'already seen => cycle' bug.
Keeps the existing 'requires exactly one trigger' (0 and 2) assertions passing alongside the new orphan check (a second trigger that is also an orphan should still report a deterministic error).
action kind:'send' still requires template_id; kind:'set_attribute' still requires key — unchanged, re-asserted to prove no regression.
testFiles.criterion: validateAutomationDefinition accepts the new hour_of_day_window + webhook nodes and rejects malformed ones; existing checks still pass; the existing 'reachable exit' test (a wait that loops to t) must be RE-WRITTEN to a non-cyclic no-exit graph because cycle-rejection now fires first.

assertions: REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp); file-local unique workspace UUID + owner user via the existing test harness/seed helper used by other local-api integration tests.
GET /workspace/settings for a fresh workspace returns settings.timezone === 'UTC' (default applied in getWorkspaceSettings like lowercase_emails/link_tracking).

PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: 'America/New_York' } returns 200 and the merged settings include timezone:'America/New_York'; a subsequent GET reads it back (persisted in workspaces.settings jsonb via the existing settings || $2::jsonb merge).
PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: 'Not/AZone' } returns 400 with an error (invalid IANA zone rejected by a server-side validator; nothing written — re-GET still shows the prior value).

PUT validates against a canonical list (Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')) with a try/new Intl.DateTimeFormat({timeZone}) fallback for engines lacking supportedValuesOf; assert a known-good odd-but-valid zone like 'Asia/Jerusalem' is ACCEPTED.

Tenant isolation: workspace_id is taken from ctx (authorizer claim), NEVER from the body — assert a body carrying a stray workspace_id does not change which row is updated (the update targets ctx.workspaceId only).

Owner-gating unchanged: the route stays mapped to capability 'manage_workspace_users' in routes.ts (assert via the existing route-capability test pattern, or a non-owner role is denied by the existing authorize path).
Merge-safety: PUT { timezone:'Europe/Paris' } does NOT clobber an existing settings.link_tracking/lowercase_emails value (jsonb merge preserves siblings).
testFiles.criterion: Workspace timezone is persisted on workspaces.settings.timezone + read back via the settings API (workspace-scoped, owner-gated); an invalid IANA zone is rejected; default is UTC.

assertions: isValidTimeZone('UTC'|'America/New_York'|'Asia/Jerusalem') === true; isValidTimeZone('Not/AZone'|''|'America/Fake') === false (used by the settings API validator).
tzOffsetMs is correct across a DST boundary for America/New_York (-5h in January, -4h in July) — assert both.

zonedInputToUtcIso('2026-03-08T02:30','America/New_York') resolves the spring-forward gap deterministically (matches the broadcast composer's two-pass re-resolution behavior) — lock the exact ISO so the extraction is behavior-preserving.
Round-trip: utcIsoToZonedInput(zonedInputToUtcIso(x, tz), tz) === x for a non-DST instant.
timeZoneList() returns a non-empty list including 'UTC' and falls back gracefully when Intl.supportedValuesOf is absent (stub it undefined).
These tests must pass IDENTICALLY whether the functions are imported from the new @cdp/shared module or still inlined — guards the extraction against drift.
testFiles.criterion: Shared tz helpers (extracted from BroadcastComposer for reuse by automation time math) are DST-correct and an IANA validator is correct — the single source of truth for automation waits/windows in later phases.

assertions: On mount, GET /workspace/settings is read and the Select is initialized to settings.timezone (or 'UTC' when unset) — assert the controlled value.
The Select options come from timeZoneList() (full IANA list) and include the persisted value even on a curated-fallback engine.

Changing the Select and saving issues PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: <chosen> }; the save control RETURNS the promise so the kit Button auto-locks/spins while in flight (assert disabled-while-pending), matching the standing button rule.
A failed PUT reverts the displayed value (optimistic rollback like toggleLinkTracking) and surfaces a toast (showToast), not a native alert.
Stable data-testid added (e.g. 'workspace-timezone-select' / 'workspace-timezone-save'); PRESERVE all existing WorkspaceSettings testids.
Mock the api client (no real network) — this is a component/unit test, not e2e.
testFiles.criterion: WorkspaceSettings renders a timezone picker (small IANA Select) wired to GET/PUT /workspace/settings; the save button auto-locks (kit Button promise behavior); invalid not selectable.

assertions: Importing { validateAutomationDefinition } plus the new node-kind discriminants from '../src/dsl.js' succeeds and index.ts re-exports them (assert the index barrel exposes the types/value guards used elsewhere).

A small type-narrowing fixture: building each node literal and passing it through validateAutomationDefinition compiles (acts as a TS strict compile guard via tsc -b; runtime assertion confirms the guard accepts the literal).

edgesOf coverage: an hour_of_day_window node contributes [next] to reachability and a webhook action contributes [next] (assert reachability/orphan detection sees these new edge sources — a window or webhook on the only path to exit keeps the graph valid).
testFiles.criterion: The new DSL types (HourOfDayWindowNode, WebhookAction shape) are exported from dsl.ts / re-exported via index.ts; the Node union includes them; typecheck-level guarantees encoded as runtime-importable shape assertions.

instructions: Read CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (automation workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline) + the cited files: §9B + CLAUDE.md (automations DSL, workspace settings, broadcast scheduler tz math). Reuse the existing automation-runner / dispatcher / segment compiler / broadcast email-instance flow / UI kit per the plan's "reuse" list.
FIRST write the failing tests from the plan (red), then implement to green. Logic in pure injected functions; handlers/components thin.

Honor every invariant — tenant isolation, workspace_id never client-supplied, all sends through the Dispatcher gating, runner idempotency/concurrency, webhook SSRF/allowlist + injected HTTP, workspace-tz time math, the single DSL graph model (no stored coords/loops/orphans), reuse + UI standing rules.

Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) or LocalStack; the integration tier uses the REAL local Postgres (never mock the DB). The webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. For UI phases verify with Playwright (real browser) against the e2e stack; seed deterministic data.

Apply any new SQL migration to BOTH cdp AND cdp_e2e (node pg script; psql unavailable). Run the relevant pnpm test (with DATABASE_URL) + pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint, and Playwright e2e for UI phases. Rebuild a changed package (tsc -b) and RESTART the dev API if you manually exercise it. Iterate until GREEN incl. a COLD run; confirm no regression. Report actual command output.
Bump the root package.json version appropriately and update CLAUDE.md if behavior/architecture changed. Implement ONLY the agreed design — no scope creep. Return ONLY the JSON.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { filesCreated: string[], filesModified: string[], migrationsApplied: string[], versionBump: string, testsPass: boolean, testSummary: string, e2eSummary: string, typecheckOk: boolean, lintOk: boolean, commandsRun: string[], notes: string }
prompt.role: senior full-stack TypeScript engineer building a serverless multi-tenant CDP automation engine + Preact SPA, test-first
prompt.task: Implement "Workspace timezone + DSL extensions (hour-window, webhook)" to satisfy its test plan and acceptance criteria. Red → green → refactor. REUSE existing infra; do not reinvent.

title: Implement: Workspace timezone + DSL extensions (hour-window, webhook) (iteration 1)

The DSL types are exported + documented; no runner/execution change yet (that is phase 2). Typecheck + lint clean; no regression in existing automation-runner unit tests.

This is the clock for all automation time math.

Extend the automation DSL (services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts) with TWO new node kinds, PURE + structurally validated: (a) an hour_of_day_window node — config: allowed hour range (startHour..endHour, 0–23) + optional allowed days-of-week; advances when already inside the window, else parks until the next window opening (computed later in the runner). (b) a webhook ACTION kind on the action node — config: url, method, headers (incl. an optional secret-backed auth header), bodyTemplate (merge-aware), timeoutMs, maxRetries; next edge after the call.

Keep it pure + exhaustively unit-tested.
phase.title: Workspace timezone + DSL extensions (hour-window, webhook)

e2eFlows: NONE this phase (phase.frontend=false; the WorkspaceSettings picker is covered by a component unit test, not Playwright). A browser e2e for the timezone picker + automation builder is deferred to a later UI phase. If a smoke e2e is desired, it would be: sign in as owner (seeded Acme) -> Workspace settings -> select a timezone -> reload -> assert it persisted; explicitly out of scope now.
implUnits: 1. (test-first) Write/extend services/automation-runner/test/node-dsl-and-validation.test.ts with the new accept/reject cases AND fix the now-cyclic 'reachable exit' fixture; add dsl-types-exports.test.ts. Run -> RED.

2. Extend services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts: add HourOfDayWindowNode interface (type:'hour_of_day_window', startHour, endHour, daysOfWeek?, next), add 'webhook' to ActionNode.kind with url/method/headers/bodyTemplate/timeoutMs/maxRetries fields (document overnight wrap-around + that secret auth headers will be envelope-encrypted at rest in phase 2), add both to the Node union and NODE_TYPES set, add validateNodeFields arms, add edgesOf arm.

3. Add detectCycle + findOrphans pure helpers and call them inside validateAutomationDefinition (after one-trigger, per-node, startNode-resolves, reachable-exit). Update the function's doc comment to list the new checks. Re-export new types via services/automation-runner/src/index.ts. Run unit tests -> GREEN.

4. Create packages/shared/src/timezone.ts by EXTRACTING tzOffsetMs/zonedInputToUtcIso/utcIsoToZonedInput/timeZoneList from BroadcastComposer + add isValidTimeZone; export from packages/shared/src/index.ts. Add packages/shared/test/timezone.test.ts. Re-import the helpers in web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx (delete the local copies) — assert no behavior change. Run -> GREEN. (Rebuild @cdp/shared via tsc -b since local-api imports from dist.)

6. Extend services/local-api/src/handlers.ts: getWorkspaceSettings adds timezone default 'UTC'; updateWorkspaceSettings adds a timezone branch that validates via isValidTimeZone (from @cdp/shared) and 400s on invalid, else merges into the settings jsonb (reuse the existing settings || $2::jsonb pattern, workspace_id from ctx only). Route stays 'manage_workspace_users'. Run integration -> GREEN. RESTART dev API after the edit.

7. Write web/test/workspace-timezone-picker.test.ts -> RED; add the picker to WorkspaceSettings.tsx (a kit Select fed by timeZoneList(), initialized from GET, saved via PUT with optimistic rollback + showToast, promise-returning save so the kit Button auto-locks; new stable testids). Run -> GREEN.

8. pnpm typecheck + pnpm lint clean; run the FULL automation-runner unit suite + local-api + web unit suites to confirm no regression. Bump root package.json (minor — new feature). Update CLAUDE.md (automations DSL section: two new node kinds + tightened validator; workspace settings: timezone setting; note tz helpers now live in @cdp/shared).
mocks: No SES/AWS in this phase (no sends executed yet) — none needed for the DSL/settings tests.
No webhook HTTP client invoked in this phase (types + structural validation only); the injected/mocked HTTP client is a PHASE-2 concern — explicitly NOT mocked here.
web picker test: mock the api client (api.get/api.put) — no real network.
@cdp/shared/timezone fallback test: stub Intl.supportedValuesOf to undefined to exercise the curated fallback.
plan.notes: PLANNING ONLY — no code/tests written here. Two gotchas the implementer must handle: (1) the EXISTING 'requires a reachable exit' unit test uses a wait node that loops back to the trigger (w.next='t'); once cycle-rejection lands, that graph throws /cycle/ before /no exit/, so the test MUST be rewritten to an acyclic-but-exitless graph — otherwise it's a false regression. (2) Cycle detection must NOT flag a legitimate diamond / re-convergence (two paths into the same downstream node) — use a DFS grey-stack (back-edge to a node on the current recursion stack), not a global 'seen' set; add an explicit diamond-accept test. Scope discipline: hour_of_day_window + webhook are TYPES + STRUCTURAL validation ONLY — NO runner execution, NO real HTTP, NO SSRF IP/host allowlist enforcement, NO secret encryption wiring (all phase 2); the only SSRF-adjacent guard here is the http(s)-scheme check in the validator. Overnight windows (startHour>endHour) are accepted as valid wrap-around (document semantics; runner resolves later). The tz extraction must be behavior-preserving (lock exact ISO outputs in the shared tz test). Apply no new SQL migration — timezone lives in the existing workspaces.settings jsonb. Bump root version (minor) and update CLAUDE.md per process hygiene. Tier mix: pure unit (DSL + tz helpers + picker component), REAL-Postgres integration (settings API), no e2e this phase.
pureFunctions: validateAutomationDefinition (EXTEND): add validation arms for hour_of_day_window (startHour/endHour integer 0-23, optional daysOfWeek subset of 0-6 unique non-empty, next edge) and action kind 'webhook' (url http(s) only, method in {GET,POST,PUT,PATCH,DELETE}, headers object, bodyTemplate string, timeoutMs>0, maxRetries>=0, next edge).
validateNodeFields (EXTEND): new switch cases for the two node shapes; webhook url scheme guard (reject non-http(s)) as a model-layer SSRF pre-check.
edgesOf (EXTEND): return [node.next] for hour_of_day_window; webhook action already covered by the existing 'action' arm returning [node.next].
hasReachableExit (KEEP): unchanged BFS — already traverses via edgesOf.

detectCycle (NEW, pure): DFS with a recursion/grey-stack over the node graph from startNode; throws on a back-edge to a node currently on the DFS stack (distinguish from a diamond re-convergence which is allowed).

findOrphans (NEW, pure): BFS/DFS reachable-set from startNode; any node id in nodes not in the reachable set is an orphan -> throw. Compose both into validateAutomationDefinition after the per-node + reachable-exit checks.
isValidTimeZone (NEW, @cdp/shared): true iff new Intl.DateTimeFormat('en-US',{timeZone}) does not throw; used by the settings API and the picker.

tzOffsetMs / zonedInputToUtcIso / utcIsoToZonedInput / timeZoneList (EXTRACT from web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx into @cdp/shared/timezone.ts): move verbatim, re-import in BroadcastComposer so automation time math reuses the SAME DST-correct implementation. Behavior-preserving extraction.
reuse: services/automation-runner/src/dsl.ts — extend in place (Node union, NODE_TYPES, validateNodeFields, edgesOf, validateAutomationDefinition, hasReachableExit). Do NOT introduce a second graph model (invariant: DSL is the single graph model).
services/automation-runner/test/node-dsl-and-validation.test.ts validDef() helper + existing assertion style — extend it; reuse @cdp/segments AstNode in the condition node (already imported).

web/src/screens/BroadcastComposer.tsx tz helpers (tzOffsetMs/zonedInputToUtcIso/utcIsoToZonedInput/timeZoneList) — extract to @cdp/shared/timezone.ts and reuse on BOTH sides (broadcast scheduler + automation time math) so there is ONE DST-correct implementation.

services/local-api/src/handlers.ts get/updateWorkspaceSettings — extend the existing settings jsonb merge (lowercase_emails/link_tracking pattern); routes.ts capability mapping ('manage_workspace_users') unchanged.

web/src/screens/WorkspaceSettings.tsx existing toggle pattern (optimistic state + savingSettings lock + rollback on catch) for the timezone picker; web/src/ui/kit.tsx Select + Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) + Field; web/src/ui/toast.tsx showToast for feedback (never native alert/confirm).

packages/db/src/secret-crypto.ts (encryptSecret/decryptSecret/isEncryptedSecret) — referenced in the webhook field DOC/comment as the phase-2 mechanism for the optional auth header secret; NOT wired this phase (types only).
packages/shared/src/index.ts barrel + project-references (tsc -b) so the new timezone module is importable by web AND local-api.

assertions: EXTEND existing file (do not replace) — keep validDef() + every current passing assertion green (one-trigger, unresolvable edge, missing startNode, per-type required fields, unknown type, resolveStartNode/findNode).

FIX the current 'requires a reachable exit' test: its `w: wait -> t` is now a CYCLE, so it would throw /cycle/ not /no exit/. Replace with a genuinely no-exit-but-acyclic graph (e.g. t->a(set_attribute)->a2(set_attribute)->... all chain to nodes that never reach an exit, or a chain ending at a node whose next is itself-forbidden) OR a chain t->w(until)->a where a.next points only back UP is removed; use a finite acyclic chain that simply contains no exit node at all.
ACCEPTS a well-formed graph containing an hour_of_day_window node: { type:'hour_of_day_window', startHour:9, endHour:17, next:'a' } wired trigger->window->send->exit, not toThrow.
ACCEPTS hour_of_day_window with optional daysOfWeek: [1,2,3,4,5] (Mon-Fri); not toThrow.
REJECTS hour_of_day_window missing startHour or endHour -> toThrow(/startHour|endHour|hour window/).
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with out-of-range hours (startHour:-1, endHour:24, or non-integer 9.5) -> toThrow(/hour/).
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with an invalid daysOfWeek (value 7, duplicate, non-array, empty array) -> toThrow(/day/).

ACCEPTS a window where startHour > endHour (overnight window, e.g. 22..6) as a VALID wrap-around range (document the semantics; runner interprets later) — assert not toThrow, so the structural gate does not forbid it.
REJECTS hour_of_day_window with a missing/unresolvable next edge -> toThrow(/next|unresolvable/).

ACCEPTS an action node with kind:'webhook' fully configured { kind:'webhook', url:'https://hooks.example.com/x', method:'POST', headers:{}, bodyTemplate:'{}', timeoutMs:5000, maxRetries:2, next:'x' } -> not toThrow.
REJECTS webhook action missing url -> toThrow(/url/).
REJECTS webhook action missing/invalid method (not one of GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE) -> toThrow(/method/).

REJECTS webhook action with a non-http(s) url scheme (e.g. file:, ftp:, javascript:) -> toThrow(/url|scheme|https?/) — structural SSRF pre-check at the model layer (full IP/host allowlist SSRF enforcement is phase 2 runtime, but the scheme guard belongs here).
REJECTS webhook action with a non-object headers, negative/zero timeoutMs, or negative maxRetries -> toThrow.
REJECTS a CYCLE / back-edge: trigger->w(wait)->c(condition onTrue:'w' onFalse:'x') where an edge points back to an ancestor -> toThrow(/cycle|back-edge/).
REJECTS a self-loop: a node whose own next targets itself -> toThrow(/cycle/).
REJECTS an ORPHAN node: a graph with a valid reachable path to an exit PLUS an extra node not reachable from startNode -> toThrow(/orphan|unreachable|not reachable/).

Confirms a diamond (condition onTrue->m, onFalse->n, both m,n -> same exit) is NOT a cycle and is ACCEPTED (a node reached via two paths is fine; only a back-edge to an ancestor is a cycle) — guards against a naive 'already seen => cycle' bug.
Keeps the existing 'requires exactly one trigger' (0 and 2) assertions passing alongside the new orphan check (a second trigger that is also an orphan should still report a deterministic error).
action kind:'send' still requires template_id; kind:'set_attribute' still requires key — unchanged, re-asserted to prove no regression.
testFiles.criterion: validateAutomationDefinition accepts the new hour_of_day_window + webhook nodes and rejects malformed ones; existing checks still pass; the existing 'reachable exit' test (a wait that loops to t) must be RE-WRITTEN to a non-cyclic no-exit graph because cycle-rejection now fires first.

assertions: REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp); file-local unique workspace UUID + owner user via the existing test harness/seed helper used by other local-api integration tests.
GET /workspace/settings for a fresh workspace returns settings.timezone === 'UTC' (default applied in getWorkspaceSettings like lowercase_emails/link_tracking).

PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: 'America/New_York' } returns 200 and the merged settings include timezone:'America/New_York'; a subsequent GET reads it back (persisted in workspaces.settings jsonb via the existing settings || $2::jsonb merge).
PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: 'Not/AZone' } returns 400 with an error (invalid IANA zone rejected by a server-side validator; nothing written — re-GET still shows the prior value).

PUT validates against a canonical list (Intl.supportedValuesOf('timeZone')) with a try/new Intl.DateTimeFormat({timeZone}) fallback for engines lacking supportedValuesOf; assert a known-good odd-but-valid zone like 'Asia/Jerusalem' is ACCEPTED.

Tenant isolation: workspace_id is taken from ctx (authorizer claim), NEVER from the body — assert a body carrying a stray workspace_id does not change which row is updated (the update targets ctx.workspaceId only).

Owner-gating unchanged: the route stays mapped to capability 'manage_workspace_users' in routes.ts (assert via the existing route-capability test pattern, or a non-owner role is denied by the existing authorize path).
Merge-safety: PUT { timezone:'Europe/Paris' } does NOT clobber an existing settings.link_tracking/lowercase_emails value (jsonb merge preserves siblings).
testFiles.criterion: Workspace timezone is persisted on workspaces.settings.timezone + read back via the settings API (workspace-scoped, owner-gated); an invalid IANA zone is rejected; default is UTC.

assertions: isValidTimeZone('UTC'|'America/New_York'|'Asia/Jerusalem') === true; isValidTimeZone('Not/AZone'|''|'America/Fake') === false (used by the settings API validator).
tzOffsetMs is correct across a DST boundary for America/New_York (-5h in January, -4h in July) — assert both.

zonedInputToUtcIso('2026-03-08T02:30','America/New_York') resolves the spring-forward gap deterministically (matches the broadcast composer's two-pass re-resolution behavior) — lock the exact ISO so the extraction is behavior-preserving.
Round-trip: utcIsoToZonedInput(zonedInputToUtcIso(x, tz), tz) === x for a non-DST instant.
timeZoneList() returns a non-empty list including 'UTC' and falls back gracefully when Intl.supportedValuesOf is absent (stub it undefined).
These tests must pass IDENTICALLY whether the functions are imported from the new @cdp/shared module or still inlined — guards the extraction against drift.
testFiles.criterion: Shared tz helpers (extracted from BroadcastComposer for reuse by automation time math) are DST-correct and an IANA validator is correct — the single source of truth for automation waits/windows in later phases.

assertions: On mount, GET /workspace/settings is read and the Select is initialized to settings.timezone (or 'UTC' when unset) — assert the controlled value.
The Select options come from timeZoneList() (full IANA list) and include the persisted value even on a curated-fallback engine.

Changing the Select and saving issues PUT /workspace/settings { timezone: <chosen> }; the save control RETURNS the promise so the kit Button auto-locks/spins while in flight (assert disabled-while-pending), matching the standing button rule.
A failed PUT reverts the displayed value (optimistic rollback like toggleLinkTracking) and surfaces a toast (showToast), not a native alert.
Stable data-testid added (e.g. 'workspace-timezone-select' / 'workspace-timezone-save'); PRESERVE all existing WorkspaceSettings testids.
Mock the api client (no real network) — this is a component/unit test, not e2e.
testFiles.criterion: WorkspaceSettings renders a timezone picker (small IANA Select) wired to GET/PUT /workspace/settings; the save button auto-locks (kit Button promise behavior); invalid not selectable.

assertions: Importing { validateAutomationDefinition } plus the new node-kind discriminants from '../src/dsl.js' succeeds and index.ts re-exports them (assert the index barrel exposes the types/value guards used elsewhere).

A small type-narrowing fixture: building each node literal and passing it through validateAutomationDefinition compiles (acts as a TS strict compile guard via tsc -b; runtime assertion confirms the guard accepts the literal).

edgesOf coverage: an hour_of_day_window node contributes [next] to reachability and a webhook action contributes [next] (assert reachability/orphan detection sees these new edge sources — a window or webhook on the only path to exit keeps the graph valid).
testFiles.criterion: The new DSL types (HourOfDayWindowNode, WebhookAction shape) are exported from dsl.ts / re-exported via index.ts; the Node union includes them; typecheck-level guarantees encoded as runtime-importable shape assertions.
